page: Vultr vbm-24c-256gb-amd is a bare metal server belonging to the Bare Metal SSD family. It features an AMD EPYC 7443P processor built on the x86_64 architecture, delivering 48 dedicated physical cores with a base clock speed of 2.85 GHz. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 5.33 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is provided by a bundled 960 GB SSD, and the configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

In benchmark evaluations, this server demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance and memory scores, placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Multi-core CPU performance is strong, ranking in the top 25% with a stress-ng multi-core score of 77709.84 and a PassMark CPU score of 60061.01. Memory bandwidth benchmarks for large block read and write operations fall within the average range, while database operations and cryptographic encryption tests show strong performance in the top 25%.

The server's hardware profile offers a highly cost-efficient alternative to virtualized environments by providing dedicated, non-shared resources. This resource density makes it highly suitable for multi-threaded workloads, database operations, and local LLM inference tasks using small to medium models. It is well-suited for deployment scenarios requiring consistent, raw compute power without hypervisor overhead, such as database hosting, heavy multi-threaded compilation, and general-purpose enterprise workloads.
description: Vultr vbm-24c-256gb-amd is a bare metal server featuring a dedicated AMD EPYC 7443P processor with 48 physical cores running at 2.85 GHz. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory and a 960 GB local SSD. This configuration delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong multi-core capabilities, as evidenced by its stress-ng and PassMark benchmark results. The server provides high resource density and dedicated hardware, making it a cost-efficient option for workloads that require consistent performance without virtualization overhead. It is highly suitable for database hosting, multi-threaded data processing, and medium-scale language model inference.
